{{External|rs|rsc=Cook's assistant}}
{{Has quick guide|speedrun=1}}
{{Infobox Quest
|name = Cook's Assistant
|number = 1
|image = [[File:Cook's Assistant.png|300px]]
|release = [[4 January]] [[2001]]
|update = Runescape beta is now online!
|members = No
|series = None
|developer = [[Paul Gower]]
}}
'''Cook's Assistant''' is the first ''[[Old School RuneScape|RuneScape]]'' [[Quests|quest]] ever released and one of the six quests available during the game's launch. In the quest, the player must help the [[Cook (Lumbridge)|cook]] of [[Lumbridge Castle]] make a birthday [[cake]] for [[Duke Horacio]].

==Details==
{{Quest details
|start = Speak to the [[Cook (Lumbridge)|Cook]] in the kitchen of [[Lumbridge Castle]].
|startmap = 3208,3214
|difficulty = Novice
|description = The Lumbridge Castle cook is in a mess. It is the Duke of Lumbridge's birthday and the cook is making the cake.

He needs a lot of ingredients and doesn't have much time.
|length = Very Short
|items = * [[Egg]] (can be obtained during the quest)
* [[Bucket of milk]]
**A [[bucket]] (if obtaining the milk during the quest)
* [[Pot of flour]]
**A [[pot]] (if obtaining the flour during the quest)
|leagueRegion = <poem>
Unlocking {{LeagueRegion|Asgarnia}} {{okay}} will auto-complete the quest
Unlocking {{LeagueRegion|Kandarin}} {{okay}} will auto-complete the quest
</poem>
}}

==Introduction==
This quest is typically among the first to be completed by new players and is intended to encourage exploration of [[Lumbridge]]. Note, however, that the majority of the quest can be skipped simply by bringing the necessary ingredients in your inventory before starting; all of the items are tradeable and can be bought from the [[Grand Exchange]]. When speaking to the cook with these items in your inventory, the player will say that they happen to have those items with them, and the cook will ask if they were planning on baking a cake, thereby completing the quest in seconds.

==Walkthrough==
===A feast for a Duke===
[[File:Cook's Assistant - Lumbridge kitchen.png|thumb|300x300px|The quest start location.]]
[[File:Cook (Lumbridge) chathead.png|left|frameless]]
Begin the quest by speaking with the Lumbridge [[Cook (Lumbridge)|cook]] found in the kitchen on the {{FloorNumber|uk=0}} of [[Lumbridge Castle]]. He mentions it is [[Duke Horacio]]'s birthday and that he is supposed to bake the Duke a [[cake]]. Unfortunately, however, he has forgotten to buy the ingredients and asks the player to bring him a [[bucket of milk]], an [[egg]], and a [[pot of flour]].

====Bucket and pot====
A [[bucket]] spawns in the cellar of Lumbridge Castle, and a [[pot]] spawns in the kitchen on the centre table. You can also purchase both a bucket and a pot from the [[Lumbridge General Store]] for {{NoCoins|2}} and {{NoCoins|1}} [[coins]], respectively.

====Egg====
[[File:Cook's Assistant - Chicken coop.png|thumb|300x300px|The location for an [[egg]], found in the coop north of [[Lumbridge]].]]
Go east out of [[Lumbridge Castle]] and run north along the path until you reach the [[chicken]] [[coop]] north-west of [[Fred the Farmer|Farmer Fred]]'s house. Go into the coop and pick up an [[egg]].

====Bucket of milk====
[[File:Cook's Assistant - Dairy cow.png|300x300px|thumb|Milking a [[dairy cow]].]]
With an empty [[bucket]], go north into the [[cow]] pasture. Use the empty bucket on a [[dairy cow]] (distinguishable by its cowbell) to get a [[bucket of milk]]. You can optionally talk to [[Gillie Groats]] for an explanation on how to milk a cow. She can be found across the [[River Lum|river]] next to the dairy cow in the eastern [[Lumbridge cow field]].

====Pot of flour====
With an empty [[pot]], leave the cow pasture and go south-west to the wheat field. Pick some [[grain]], and then enter the [[Mill Lane Mill]], found up the road to the north-east. Climb to the top floor, put the grain in the hopper, then pull the lever and climb back down to the {{FloorNumber|uk=0}}. Finally, use an empty pot on the [[flour bin]] (which is now full of flour) to get a [[pot of flour]]. You can optionally talk to [[Millie Miller]] inside the mill for an explanation on how to make [[pot of flour|flour]].

===Delivery===
After collecting the ingredients, speak to the [[Cook (Lumbridge)|cook]] to deliver them.

'''Congratulations, quest complete!'''

==Rewards==
{{Quest rewards
|name = Cook's Assistant
|image=[[File:Cook's Assistant reward scroll.png|centre]]
|qp = 1
|rewards = 
* {{SCP|Cooking|300|link=yes}} [[experience]]
* Permission to use the [[Cooking range (Lumbridge Castle)|Cook-o-matic 100]], which reduces the chance of burning some [[food]]s.
}}

==Required for completing==
The completion of Cook's Assistant is a requirement to complete the following:
* [[Recipe for Disaster]]
* [[Easy Lumbridge & Draynor Diary]]: ''Bake some bread on the Lumbridge castle kitchen range.''

==Transcript==
{{Hastranscript|quest}}

==Changes==
{{Subject changes header}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 14 January 2026
|update = New Player Improvements Round 2
|change = The quest's journal entry was updated to make it more intuitive.
}}
{{Subject changes footer}}

==Trivia==
* There is an [[wikipedia:Easter_egg_(media)|easter egg]] in the [[Death to the Dorgeshuun]] quest. When you introduce [[Zanik]] to the [[Cook (Lumbridge)|cook]], he tries to offer the quest to her, saying, "In fact, it's a good thing I met you. It's the Duke's birthday today, and I should be making him a lovely big birthday cake. I need eggs, flour, and milk..." The player then tells the cook that Zanik does not have time for that, to which he responds, "It was worth a try." This insinuates that the cook asks all newcomers to bring him supplies.
* During the [[20th Anniversary event]], it was revealed that the cook has attempted and failed to bake a cake countless times, explaining his recurring need for ingredients from new adventurers.
* The [[Cook (servant)|cook]] at the [[Servants' Guild]] of [[Ardougne]] references this quest in her dialogue, saying, "Overnight I was fired and replaced by some fool who can't even bake a cake without help!"

{{Cook's Assistant}}
